activities_projects.title: Pressure and Area Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Understand how pressure changes with area
steps: Place a book flat on the sponge and observe the indentation
Now place the book vertically on its edge
Compare how deep the sponge is pressed
Explain how area affects pressure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that smaller area produces greater pressure

activities_projects.title: Liquid Pressure Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Understand that liquid pressure increases with depth
steps: Make three holes at different heights on a plastic bottle
Fill the bottle with water
Observe water flow from each hole
Compare the distance the water travels
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that liquid pressure increases with depth

activities_projects.title: Air Pressure Demonstration
steps: Fill the glass with water
Place cardboard over the mouth of the glass
Invert the glass carefully
Observe that the cardboard holds the water

activities_projects.title: Wind Direction Observation
steps: Attach paper strips to a stick
Place it outside in an open area
Observe the direction in which strips move
Record observations at different times of day
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns how wind direction can be observed

activities_projects.title: Balloon Air Pressure Activity
steps: Inflate one balloon and keep another uninflated
Connect them using a straw
Observe the transfer of air
Explain why the balloons change size
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands air moves from high pressure to low pressure

activities_projects.title: Paper Strip Airflow Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Observe pressure change due to fast-moving air
steps: Hold a strip of paper so it hangs freely
Blow air over the top of the strip
Observe the movement of the paper
Explain the reason for the movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that fast-moving air lowers pressure

activities_projects.title: Wind Speed Comparison
steps: Attach ribbon to a stick
Observe its movement on calm and windy days
Record the observations
Compare wind strength
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that wind speed varies due to pressure differences

activities_projects.title: Storm Impact Research
steps: Research one recent storm or cyclone
Note its location and effects
Write how people were affected
Suggest safety measures
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects scientific concepts with real-world events

activities_projects.title: Cyclone Formation Model
steps: Draw a diagram showing warm air rising and cool air moving in
Label pressure differences
Show rotation due to Earth's movement
Explain the cyclone formation process
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the process of cyclone formation

activities_projects.title: Weather Observation Diary
steps: Record wind, clouds, and temperature each day for a week
Note strong wind or calm days
Compare patterns
Relate observations to pressure differences
